title: Desmos robinsonii
taxonomy: Desmos robinsonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the spleenwort family. It is a type of fern that belongs to the genus Desmos. Desmos robinsonii is a tropical species native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji. The classification of Desmos robinsonii is further specified as a member of the family Polypodiaceae within the class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and division Pteridophyta. This classification places it among the vascular plants, which are characterized by the presence of xylem and phloem tissues.

intro: Desmos robinsonii, also known as Robinson's Desmos, is a plant species endemic to the tropical rainforests of Papua New Guinea, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Desmos robinsonii include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
